revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[Emperor Korei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [http://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref>
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[Shinto shrine]]s in [[Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>
==References== {{reflist}}
[[Category:People of Yayoi-period Japan]]
[[ja:吉備津彦命]]

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[Emperor Korei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [http://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref>
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[Shinto shrine]]s in [[Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>
==References== {{reflist}}
[[Category:People of Yayoi-period Japan]]
[[fr:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] [[ja:吉備津彦命]]

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[:en:Japan|Japan]]ese prince.  

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō. 

His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[:en:Japan|Japan]]ese prince.  

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō. 

His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[ja:吉備津彦命]][[zh:吉備津彥命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[ja:吉備津彦命]][[zh:吉備津彥命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[ja:吉備津彦命]][[zh:吉備津彥命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:吉備津彦 Kibitsuhiko 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|children={{ill|Prince Hikosashima|WD=Q11487957}}; {{ill|包媛|WD=Q106381698}}|father={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041}}|mother={{ill|Jamatonokunikahime|WD=Q97539246}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572}}; {{ill|Prince Hikosashima|WD=Q11487957}}; {{ill|Prince Wakatakehiko|WD=Q11596350}}; {{ill|Princess Chichihayahime|WD=Q10904825}}; {{ill|Princess Yamatototowakayahime|WD=Q11387099}}; {{ill|Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|WD=Q11387098}}|resting_place={{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11364273}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65318]] Wikidata name: Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65318]] Wikidata name: Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary [[:en:Japan|Japan]]ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]].<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[ja:吉備津彦命]][[zh:吉備津彥命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[ja:吉備津彦命]][[zh:吉備津彥命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[fr:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]][[ja:吉備津彦命]][[zh:吉備津彥命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:吉備津彦 Kibitsuhiko 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|children={{ill|Prince Hikosashima|WD=Q11487957}}; {{ill|包媛|WD=Q106381698}}|father={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041}}|mother={{ill|Jamatonokunikahime|WD=Q97539246}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572}}; {{ill|Prince Hikosashima|WD=Q11487957}}; {{ill|Prince Wakatakehiko|WD=Q11596350}}; {{ill|Princess Chichihayahime|WD=Q10904825}}; {{ill|Princess Yamatototowakayahime|WD=Q11387099}}; {{ill|Yamatototohimomosohime no mikoto|WD=Q11387098}}|resting_place={{ill|Nakayama Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11364273}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65318]] Wikidata name: Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65318]] Wikidata name: Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}.<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>
== Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}}

{{nihongo|'''Kibitsu-hiko-no-mikoto'''|吉備津彦命||}}, also known as '''Hiko Isuseri-hiko no mikoto,''' was a legendary {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}ese prince.
According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', he was the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}.<ref>Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref> Legend says this prince slayed an ogre called Ura, which may be the demonization of the [[Kingdom of Kibi]], destroyed during the reign of Kōrei-Tennō.
His ''[[kami]]'' is enshrined at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}s in [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)|Okayama Prefecture]] and [[Kibitsu Shrine (Bingo)|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsuhiko-jinja/ Kibitsuhiko jinja]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja-bingo/ Kibitsu jinja (Bingo)]; [http://www.kotodamaya.com/kibitsu-jinja/ Kibitsu jinja (Bitchu)]; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref>
